text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a software development practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. This process is automated through CI pipelines, which perform automated builds and run tests every time new code is added. The main goal is to catch issues early, before they become larger problems. By integrating small changes regularly, teams can detect bugs, code conflicts, or failures immediately. This helps maintain high-quality software and reduces the time spent fixing errors later. Overall, CI enables faster, smoother development while improving collaboration and reliability in projects.
